name: Origin of fermion chirality
summary: All Standard Model fermions are chiral: left-handed leptons and quarks form SU(2)_L doublets, right-handed ones are singlets, and Dirac mass terms are forbidden by SM gauge invariance until electroweak symmetry breaks. Chirality is a structural property — anomaly cancellation between quark and lepton hypercharges is non-trivially required for SM consistency. Why nature is chiral, why each generation has the precise hypercharges that cancel anomalies, and why right-handed neutrinos are absent from the SM, are all open structural questions.

characteristic_numbers.expected: non-zero would break SU(2)_L gauge invariance via anomalies
characteristic_numbers.note: Anomaly cancellation between lepton-doublet and right-handed-electron hypercharges is required by SM consistency.

characteristic_numbers.expected: non-zero would break U(1)_Y at quantum level
characteristic_numbers.note: Forces the specific hypercharge assignments {1/6, 2/3, -1/3, -1/2, -1} per generation - one of the deepest 'why these numbers' SM questions.

principles_stressed.note: Anomaly cancellation forces the specific SM hypercharge pattern and forbids vector-like extensions that would break gauge invariance. Suggests either a GUT origin (SU(5), SO(10), Pati-Salam, E6 force the chiral structure) or a string-theoretic constraint.

history: Recognised since the SM was constructed (Glashow-Weinberg-Salam 1961-1968). Anomaly cancellation worked out by Bouchiat-Iliopoulos-Meyer (1972), 'An anomaly-free version of Weinberg's model', PLB 38, 519. SO(10) embedding (Georgi 1975, Fritzsch-Minkowski 1975) shows all fermions in a single 16 — the natural unification. String-theoretic constraints from N=1 SUSY in 4D after Calabi-Yau compactification (Candelas-Horowitz-Strominger-Witten 1985) pick out specific chiral matter content.

references.citation: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer, 'An anomaly-free version of Weinberg's model', Phys. Lett. B 38, 519 (1972)
